name: Uranium Sector
summary: Day 62 Wednesday. URA $54.23 WED (-4.4% 1D, +16.3% 1M, -8.6% 3M) — SHARP REVERSAL ON OPENAI-Q1-MISS-LED AI-DATA-CENTER-DEMAND DOUBT; APR 22 PIVOT TOP SELL SIGNAL CONFIRMED. The same OpenAI Q1 user/revenue miss + CFO Friar compute-funding-worry that drove SMH -3.0% Wed flowed through to URA -4.4% — uranium's AI-data-center-demand thesis runs through SMR uranium contracts and Nvidia capex chain; AI doubt = uranium doubt. ~10% crisis / ~90% AI-thesis unwind decomposition. The 45%/55% crisis-vs-structural split from prior weeks framework is now DOMINATED by AI-cycle reset. Crisis premium component (energy-security proxy on Brent breaching $115 intraday) is intact but dominated by AI-cycle on Wed selling. For investor: URA -4.4% Wed is CONFIRMATION of Apr 22 pivot-top sell signal that prior framework had at watching. Pure de-escalation would produce another 10-15% retracement on top of Wed's drop given AI-thesis component is decoupled from crisis. Branch 1 reactivation (Brent sustained $115+) is the only crisis path that would reactivate URA's energy-security premium independent of AI-cycle. Mag 7 earnings tonight (MSFT/META/GOOGL/AMZN) will determine whether AI-capex doubt deepens (URA tests $50 support) or reverses (URA bounces $58+). Iran-nuclear-facility strike tail still REPORTED (CNN single-source on Vahidi-targeting; Türkiye Today single-source on Pentagon Hormuz strike plans). Structural nuclear-capacity-tripling commitments intact. URA is one of the few crisis-linked ETFs that's UP today on Brent at $109+: AI-power demand + energy-security proxy + structural nuclear-capacity-tripling commitments. The 45%/55% crisis-vs-structural split from yesterday's framework is empirically intact: crisis premium component restored on Brent reversal up; AI-structural floor unchanged. Primary chain: (a) higher-for-longer Brent (Goldman raised late-2026 forecast to $90 from $80) = energy-security premium reactivates, (b) Iran-nuclear-facility strike tail still REPORTED (CNN single-source on Vahidi-targeting; Türkiye Today single-source on Pentagon Hormuz strike plans), (c) AI-structural demand intact — SK Hynix HBM4 mass production, Samsung Q1 prelim record, Nvidia capex chain, nuclear-capacity-tripling commitments, IRA tax credits, fuel-supply programs. For investor: URA +21.4% 1M is priced for both crisis and structural; Branch 1 reactivation (Brent $115 break) would produce another +10-15% leg up; pure de-escalation would produce 10-15% retracement but AI-nuclear structural floor intact.

signals.content: URA +7.4% to $57.73 on high volume (1M +19.9%, 3M +1.3%) on a day of $9/bbl Brent intraday reversal — clean signal of blended crisis-plus-AI-structural narrative. Trump's strike-list rhetoric specifically named 'essential infrastructure including bridges and power stations' (intel A1) — implicit Iran nuclear-facility targeting framing. Explicit 'seize Iran's enriched uranium' framing has appeared in administration-channel wires (Fortune, 24/7 Wall St). Iran nuclear infrastructure now explicitly in escalation framing for first time since March. Uranium supply-security premium re-loading. (Sources: yfinance, Fortune, 24/7 Wall St)

AI-structural component (~55% per markets.md C4): SK Hynix Thursday announcement of mass production of HBM4 for Nvidia's advanced AI processors; AI data center capex compounding with hyperscalers guiding 2026 spend >$300B aggregate; URA had $3.8-4.6B inflows during 2025; government incentives (IRA tax credits, fuel-supply programs, liability protections); nuclear-capacity-tripling global commitments. The crisis-plus-AI blend means de-escalation produces 10-15% retracement but the structural AI-nuclear thesis underpins a floor. (Sources: SK Hynix, IRA documentation, Fortune $650B AI-economy framing)

URA -2.2% TO $56.47 — PROFIT-TAKING MIRROR CONFIRMS 45%/55% CRISIS-VS-STRUCTURAL SPLIT [CONFIRMED]. URA faded -2.2% Friday after Thursday's +7.4% surge; net two-session +5.0%, monthly still +17.2%. Primary chain (~65% crisis unwind, ~35% AI-structural hold): (a) Trump rhetoric softening ('don't rush me') = strike-imminence premium unwind; (b) no Iranian nuclear-facility kinetic; (c) mechanical profit-taking after +7.4% spike. AI-structural demand unchanged — the 35% of Thursday's move that was AI-structural holds. The 45%/55% crisis-vs-structural split in Thursday's move IS CONFIRMED by the one-day unwind: pure crisis premium unwound; structural floor held. For investor: URA 1M +17.2% has priced in much of Iran nuclear-facility thesis already; de-escalation produces 10-15% retracement from here but structural AI-nuclear floor intact.

edges.context: Iran's nuclear facilities are explicitly in Trump's strike-list framing; 'seize enriched uranium' rhetoric in administration circulars. Iran's enrichment infrastructure and any kinetic on it would dramatically tighten global enrichment market.
Trump's articulated strike-list (bridges, power stations) implicates Iran's nuclear infrastructure. 'Seize enriched uranium' framing has been in administration channels (Fortune).
IRA tax credits, fuel-supply programs, liability protections, US government nuclear-capacity-tripling commitment. Strong US policy underpin for uranium sector independent of crisis.
Nuclear is a complement-substitute to fossil fuel energy generation. Uranium supply-security premium re-loads with energy supply disruption.
Nuclear capacity needed for AI data center power demand — intersects with the AI chip rally (NASDAQ +1.6% record, SMH +2.6%, KOSPI record on SK Hynix HBM-Nvidia mass production).

trigger_points.condition: Confirmed US/Israeli kinetic action on Iran nuclear-enrichment facility (Natanz, Fordow, Arak)
trigger_points.mechanism: Trump's strike-list rhetoric specifically named 'essential infrastructure' — a confirmed kinetic on Iran's nuclear-enrichment facility would activate the full crisis-driven uranium thesis (URA +15-25% on the print) and dramatically tighten global enrichment market. Structurally cleaner than the current 45% crisis / 55% AI blend.

trigger_points.condition: URA breaks $62 sustained for 3 sessions (above 1M peak +25% from baseline)
trigger_points.mechanism: Sustained break above $62 confirms the crisis-plus-AI thesis has crowded into uranium beyond AI-structural support — would imply pricing of confirmed Iran nuclear targeting before official confirmation. Asymmetric retracement risk on de-escalation.

price_snapshots.trigger: Crisis-plus-AI rally on Trump strike-list rhetoric + SK Hynix HBM4 mass production announcement
price_snapshots.delta_context: +7.4% single-day move on a day of Brent intraday $9/bbl reversal; 1M +19.9%, 3M +1.3% (back to 3M start = steep-recent trajectory)

price_snapshots.trigger: Tue +2.5% 1D — UP on Brent reversing UP to $109; energy-security + AI-power proxy holding bid
price_snapshots.delta_context: +2.5% 1D, +21.4% 1M, -8.3% 3M. Tuesday's intraday move is one of the only crisis-linked equity moves UP on Brent reversal. CORRECTION: yesterday's $100.47 Brent print was yfinance contract-roll glitch; live tape Mon $108.23 / Tue $109.39. Goldman raised late-2026 Brent forecast to $90 from $80 — institutionalizing higher-for-longer; URA is structurally LONG that thesis. Crisis premium component restored; AI-structural floor unchanged. Branch 1 reactivation (Brent $115 break) would add +10-15% leg up; pure de-escalation 10-15% retracement but AI-nuclear floor intact.
